info.name: Auth – Login (POST /api/v1/auth/login)
info.description:
E2E tests for POST /api/v1/auth/login.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-login):
  1. setup          — register a fresh user, fetch OTP from Gmail, verify email
  2. validation     — 422/413 input-error cases (no setup required)
  3. happy-path     — 200 + access_token body + refresh_token cookie checks
  4. failures       — wrong password, unknown identifier, unverified account
  5. rate-limiting  — IP limiter exhaustion: 5 warmups, 6th → 429

All test users use @e2e.test or the fixed gmail_test_email address.
Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before this collection.

Notes on what is NOT covered here (already covered by internal tests):
  - Cookie MaxAge derived from expires_at       — handler_test.go TestHandler_Login_CookieMaxAgeDerivedFromRefreshExpiry
  - Secure flag when SecureCookies=true          — handler_test.go TestHandler_Login_CookieSecureTrue_WhenSecureCookiesEnabled
  - Plain ErrLoginLocked without Retry-After     — handler_test.go
  - Zero RetryAfter clamped to 1                 — handler_test.go
  - Token mint failure → 500                     — handler_test.go TestHandler_Login_TokenMintError
  - All error responses have code + JSON CT      — handler_test.go

Notes on what is NOT covered here (requires special DB setup or seed scripts):
  - Valid username + password → 200              — username not settable via public register API;
                                                   store_test.go covers the DB lookup path.
                                                   Needs a Makefile target that seeds a username row.
  - Inactive account → 403 account_inactive      — requires UPDATE users SET is_active=false in DB.
                                                   Add a seed script or Makefile target to cover this.
  - Admin-locked → 423 account_locked            — requires UPDATE users SET is_locked=true in DB.
                                                   Add a seed script or Makefile target to cover this.
  - Time-locked → 429 login_locked + Retry-After — achievable by hammering wrong passwords until
                                                   login_locked_until is set; depends on MaxLoginAttempts
                                                   config. Defer until that constant is exposed in .env.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// The rate-limiting folder overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 via its own prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the OTP from Gmail, and verifies the email. The happy-path and failures folders depend on lgn_happy_email / lgn_happy_password being set here and email_verified=true in the DB.

exec: // Use the real Gmail address so the verification OTP lands in the inbox
// that gmail_access_token can read. The address is cleaned by _e2e-db-clean
// before each run so this register never 409 on re-runs.
console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
// Placed in the test script (not prerequest) because pm.sendRequest is async.
// The callback executes during the --delay-request idle window before the
// next request fires, so 'lgn_code' is set in time.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "lgn_code":', match[1]);
// Validate that the Gmail fetch in the register test script completed.
console.error('[ERROR] collection variable "lgn_code" is empty — Gmail fetch did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set and not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');

item.name: validation
item.description: All input-validation cases for POST /login. Validation fires before any DB lookup or service call, so no pre-existing user is required. Each case expects 422 validation_error (or 413 for oversized body). Validation rules from validators.go: identifier is required and must be ≤254 bytes after trimming; password is required. Password strength is intentionally NOT re-validated on login to avoid leaking enumeration info.

exec: // validateLoginRequest trims the identifier before checking emptiness,
// so '   ' becomes '' and must return 422 validation_error.
// Password strength is NOT re-validated on login, but an empty password
// is rejected by ErrPasswordEmpty before the service is ever called.
// Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit.
// http.MaxBytesReader fires during json.Decoder.Decode and maps to 413.

item.name: happy-path
item.description: Uses the verified account created by the setup folder. Asserts that a successful login returns 200 with an access_token in the JSON body and a refresh_token Set-Cookie header with the required security attributes.

item.name: POST /login valid email+password → 200 with access_token and refresh_token cookie

exec: // No credential fields should leak into the 200 body.
pm.test('body does not contain password field', () => pm.expect(body).to.not.have.property('password'));
// refresh_token cookie must be present in the Set-Cookie response header.
// pm.response.cookies.get() does not expose HttpOnly / SameSite attributes,
// so we parse the raw Set-Cookie header for the security attributes that
// Stash the access_token for any downstream requests in this run.
// validateLoginRequest lowercases the identifier when it contains '@'.
// Sending the email in UPPERCASE must resolve to the same verified account.
pm.test('status is 200 (email normalised to lowercase before lookup)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: POST /login identifier with surrounding whitespace → 200 (trimmed before lookup)

exec: // validateLoginRequest trims whitespace from identifier before service call.
// '  email@...  ' must resolve to the same verified account.
pm.test('status is 200 (identifier trimmed before service call)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: failures
item.description: Credential-failure and account-state failure paths. Wrong password and unknown identifier are tested together to verify the anti-enumeration contract (identical 401 body in both cases). Unverified account is tested using a freshly registered but unverified user — no email fetch or verification step needed.

exec: // Valid, verified user — but wrong password.
// Service calls CheckPassword, it fails, IncrementLoginFailuresTx is called,
// Stash the body for the anti-enumeration check below.
// Use a timestamped email guaranteed not to exist in DB.
// No-rows path: GetUserForLogin returns ErrUserNotFound, service falls
// through dummy bcrypt compare, then returns ErrInvalidCredentials.
// Response must be IDENTICAL to the wrong-password case — no 404.
// Anti-enumeration: body shape must match the wrong-password response exactly.
// Register a fresh user but do NOT verify the email.
// The next test uses this account to trigger the 403 email_not_verified path.
// Timestamp-unique email so re-runs after _e2e-db-clean never 409.
// User registered but never called POST /verify-email.
// This also confirms the new user row has email_verified=false and is_active=true
pm.test('not account_locked (423) — is_locked=false on new users', () => {

item.name: time-lock
item.description:
Verifies the time-based login lockout: 10 consecutive wrong passwords set login_locked_until = NOW() + 15 minutes (IncrementLoginFailures SQL). The 11th request with the CORRECT password returns 429 login_locked because the lockout check (service step 5) fires after the password check passes but before the email_verified guard (step 6). This means a plain @e2e.test user works — no Gmail OTP fetch or email verification required.

IP strategy: the collection-level prerequest assigns a unique X-Forwarded-For IP per request, so the 11 requests in this folder never share an IP bucket and the lgn:ip: rate limiter (burst=5) is never hit. Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: // Timestamp-unique email so re-runs after _e2e-db-clean never 409.
// No verification needed: the lockout guard fires after password check
// passes (service step 5), before the email_verified check (step 6).

item.name: wrong password 1/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 1/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 2/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 2/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 3/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 3/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 4/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 4/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 5/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 5/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 6/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 6/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 7/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 7/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 8/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 8/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 9/10 — increment counter (→ 401, not yet locked)

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (attempt 9/10, not locked yet)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: wrong password 10/10 — threshold reached, login_locked_until set (→ 401)

exec: // The 10th wrong password triggers IncrementLoginFailures in the DB:
// But this request still returns 401 — the lockout check (service step 5)
// only fires on subsequent requests where the PASSWORD IS CORRECT.
pm.test('status is 401 (10th wrong password — threshold hit, counter now at 10)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
pm.test('code is invalid_credentials (not login_locked — wrong pw path)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().code).to.eql('invalid_credentials'));

item.name: correct password after lockout → 429 login_locked + Retry-After

exec: // Password is correct this time — service step 4 passes.
// Step 5: login_locked_until is in the future → LoginLockedError → 429.
// Step 6 (email_not_verified) is never reached — lockout fires first.

item.name: rate-limiting
item.description: Verifies the lgn:ip: IP rate limiter (burst=12, rate=12/15min). The folder prerequest pins _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests share one bucket. 12 warmups drain the burst capacity; the 13th must return 429 with code=too_many_requests and Retry-After: 75 (ceil(1/rate) = ceil(75) = 75s). Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env and Redis flushed (make _e2e-kv-clean) before this folder.

exec: // Override the collection-level unique IP so every request in this
// folder shares the 127.0.0.1 bucket and exhausts it together.

item.name: warmup 12/12 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (lgn:ip: slot 12 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by the rate-limiting folder prerequest.
Set by setup folder from gmail_test_email environment variable. Reused by happy-path and failures folders.
Set by setup folder. Used for happy-path and wrong-password failure test.
OTP fetched from Gmail. Set asynchronously by the setup/register test script during the --delay-request window.
access_token from a successful login. Stored by the happy-path test for potential downstream use.
JSON-stringified body from the wrong-password 401. Used by the anti-enumeration check to assert identical shape from the unknown-identifier 401.
Timestamp-unique email for the unverified-account failure test. Set by the failures/setup-unverified-user request.
Timestamp-unique email for the time-lock test. Set by the time-lock/setup request. No email verification needed — lockout fires before the email_verified guard.
Password for lgn_timelock_email. Sent as the 11th request (correct password) to trigger 429 login_locked.
